1: Motor overload — current exceeds safe limit, check for jams or bearing wear
2: Temperature high — cooling may be blocked, reduce load or check fans
3: Conveyor jam — product stuck at sensors, clear obstruction before restart
4: Sensor failure — photoeye not responding, check wiring and alignment
5: Communication loss — fieldbus timeout, check cables and network switch
6: VFD fault — variable frequency drive tripped, check VFD display for code
7: Low pressure — pneumatic supply below threshold, check compressor
8: E-Stop active — emergency stop engaged, verify area is safe before reset
9: Guard door open — safety enclosure not closed, close door to enable restart
10: Belt slip — motor running but belt not moving, check tension
11: Overtravel — actuator exceeded limit switch, check for mechanical obstruction
12: Phase loss — three-phase power imbalance, check incoming supply
13: Ground fault — leakage current detected, inspect motor insulation
14: PLC battery low — backup battery needs replacement
15: Watchdog timeout — PLC scan time exceeded, check program complexity
20: VFD overcurrent (OC) — motor drawing too much, check for jam or reduce load
21: VFD overvoltage (OV) — regen or input spike, check decel ramp and input power
22: VFD undervoltage (UV) — input power sag or loss, check supply voltage
23: VFD overtemperature — drive or motor too hot, check fan and reduce duty cycle
24: VFD ground fault — motor winding or cable fault, megger test motor and cable
25: VFD input phase loss — missing phase on input, check 3-phase supply
26: VFD output phase loss — motor or cable open phase, check motor terminals and cable
27: VFD comms loss — Modbus timeout, check Ethernet cable to VFD
28: VFD external fault — input from PLC/safety relay, check E-stop and safety chain
29: VFD unknown fault — check VFD display panel for details
